raw:
{
  "judge_id": "J001",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.4", "R1.7", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {
    "R1.4": 5,
    "R1.7": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.4": "The output maps against the nearest 3-5 works (MetaGPT, ChatDev, AutoGen) and identifies sharp vs rhetorical delta.",
    "R1.7": "The output audits every load-bearing citation; flags missing key works with bibliographic precision.",
    "RX.1": "For each recommendation, marks confidence and missing evidence, such as the need to verify citations and baseline numbers.",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) without any drift.",
    "RX.4": "No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original, such as the false novelty claim and under-specified comparison.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, including detailed notes on what was changed and why."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.4": 0.9,
    "R1.7": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.4: The output maps against the nearest 3-5 works (MetaGPT, ChatDev, AutoGen) and identifies sharp vs rhetorical delta.
rationale.R1.7: The output audits every load-bearing citation; flags missing key works with bibliographic precision.
rationale.RX.1: For each recommendation, marks confidence and missing evidence, such as the need to verify citations and baseline numbers.
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) without any drift.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; identifies harmful patterns in the original, such as the false novelty claim and under-specified comparison.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason, including detailed notes on what was changed and why.
